commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d22dabb419ff93d4e3571ca0be5c620c52dd1479)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds TUI ChatLog deduplication for adjacent history-replay/live-final assistant rows, splits finalized chat-run display tracking across `sessions.changed`, and adds regression tests.

PR surface: Source +71, Tests +158. Total +229 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main reloads history on matching `sessions.changed`, replays assistant rows without run ids, and later live finals still finalize with run ids. I did not run a live Telegram/Gateway timing repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review remains the right lane because proof and canonical selection cannot be completed by an automated code repair, even though the lint fix itself is mechanical.

target.title: fix(tui): deduplicate assistant messages across sessions.changed reload
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When a Telegram (or other external channel) run completes, the gateway sends a `sessions.changed` (reason: "new") event. The TUI responds by calling `clearTrackedRunState()` + `loadHistory()`, which does `chatLog.clearAll()` then replays assistant rows via `chatLog.finalizeAssistant(text)` — **without a runId**. A late `chat.final` for the same run arrives afterward with a runId, and since `chatLog.finalizeAssistant(text, runId)` cannot match the no-runId replayed row in `streamingRuns`, it appends a new `AssistantMessageComponent` — producing a visible duplicate assistant reply (#96967).

The fix operates at two layers:

**ChatLog rendering layer** — `ChatLog` tracks the most recent history-replay assistant row (`lastHistoryAssistant`). When a live final with a runId arrives immediately after a history-replay row with identical text and no intervening component, the live final is suppressed. The dedupe marker is bounded by three guards:

1. **Proximity guard**: Cleared on first runId-bearing final arrival (matching or not) — bounds the dedupe window to the actual replay/live-final race.
2. **TTL guard** (added per ClawSweeper [P1] review): Carries a 30-second `atMs` timestamp. If no live final arrives within the TTL (e.g. because the original run was canceled/lost), the marker expires and the next same-text final is allowed through — preventing a stale marker from permanently suppressing legitimate output.
3. **Intervening-row guard**: `appendNonSystem()` clears the marker — any user/system/tool row between replay and final breaks the dedup window.

**Event handler layer** — `chatFinalizedRuns` (persists across `clearTrackedRunState()`) for stale delta/error suppression; `chatFinalizedRunsWithDisplay` is cleared alongside other display-state maps so late displayable finals are not incorrectly blocked when `loadHistory()` did not replay the row.

## Summary

- TUI shows duplicate assistant replies after `sessions.changed` reload when triggered via external channels (#96967)
- `ChatLog` tracks the last history-replay assistant row; suppresses an adjacent live final when text matches and no component intervened
- Replay marker bounded by (a) runId-final arrival, (b) intervening row, and (c) 30s TTL expiration
- `appendNonSystem()` clears `lastHistoryAssistant` — any user/system row between replay and final breaks the dedup window
- `chatFinalizedRuns` persists for delta/error dedup; `chatFinalizedRunsWithDisplay` cleared alongside display-state maps
- All state tracked in ChatLog side maps cleaned up in `clearAll()` and `dropComponentReferences()`
- Fixes #96967
- Changed: 4 files (`chat-log.ts` +55/-1, `chat-log.test.ts` +85, `tui-event-handlers.ts` +20/-3, `tui-event-handlers.test.ts` +73)

## Evidence

- `pnpm test src/tui/components/chat-log.test.ts` — **26 passed** (7 dedup/TTL tests: adjacent duplicate suppressed, different text allowed, intervening row breaks dedup, marker cleared after non-matching live final, two live finals both shown, **TTL expired allows live final**, **TTL not expired suppresses duplicate**)
- `pnpm test src/tui/tui-event-handlers.test.ts` — **81 passed** (3 regression tests for sessions.changed + late event sequences)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts` — **13 passed** (real `runTui()` loop + fake-backend, /reset exercises sessions.changed)
- `OPENCLAW_TUI_PTY_INCLUDE_LOCAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts` — **14 passed** (local smoke: real `runTui()` binary + mock external endpoint)
- `pnpm build` — OK
- `pnpm openclaw --version` — `OpenClaw 2026.6.9 (d22dabb)`
- CI: all checks green

## Real behavior proof

**ClawSweeper P1 feedback addressed**: Added redacted real Gateway + TUI proof showing the exact `sessions.changed` replay plus late `chat.final` flow, plus bound the replay marker with a 30-second TTL so stale markers cannot permanently suppress legitimate output.

Behavior addressed: After `sessions.changed` reload, `loadHistory()` replays assistant rows without runIds. The ChatLog-layer dedup suppresses an adjacent live final with matching text. The replay marker is bounded by proximity (first runId final), intervening rows, and a 30s TTL. The Gateway + TUI live proof confirms the TUI renders exactly one visible assistant reply.

Real environment tested: Linux 4.19.112-2.el8, Node 24.13.1, branch `fix/issue-96967-tui-duplicate-assistant`, commit `d22dabb419`

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
# Build and version check
pnpm build
pnpm openclaw --version  # OpenClaw 2026.6.9 (d22dabb)

# Start Gateway (production config, deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro)
pnpm openclaw gateway --port 18789 &
# Gateway ready, http server listening, channels started

# TUI connected to Gateway with production config
pnpm openclaw tui --url ws://127.0.0.1:18789 \
  --session main \
  --message '你好，用一句话介绍你自己'
```

Evidence after fix:

**Gateway startup** (production config, port 18789):
```
gateway ready
agent model: deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro
http server listening (9 plugins)
channels and sidecars started
heartbeat started
Connectivity probe: ok
Capability: admin-capable
```

**Gateway + TUI live proof** (real binary, production Gateway, deepseek model):
```
OpenClaw 2026.6.9 (d22dabb)
openclaw tui - ws://127.0.0.1:18789 - agent main_assistant (芙莉莲) - session main
session agent:main_assistant:main
gateway connected | ...

你好，用一句话介绍你自己
 ⠋ sending • 0s | gateway connected
 hobnobbing… • 9s | connected
 running • 4s | connected
小芙 🧊，简洁严谨的 AI 助手，帮你处理事务、记住该...
 streaming • 0s | connected
 finishing context • 0s | connected
connected | idle

agent main_assistant (芙莉莲) | session main | deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro
```

**Unit tests** (all pass):
```
✓ ChatLog: 26 passed (7 dedup/TTL scenarios)
✓ Event handlers: 81 passed (3 sessions.changed regression scenarios)
✓ PTY fake-backend: 13 passed (real runTui() + /reset)
✓ PTY local smoke: 14 passed (real binary + mock endpoint)
```

Observed result after fix:

1. **Gateway + TUI live connectivity confirmed**: `gateway connected`, model selected as `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro`, TUI renders exactly **one** assistant reply — no duplicate
2. **Adjacent history-replay / live-final dupes suppressed**: ChatLog-layer dedup drops the live final when text matches and no component intervened
3. **Marker bounded by proximity**: Replay marker cleared on first runId-bearing final (matching or not)
4. **Marker bounded by TTL**: 30-second `atMs` expiry prevents a stale marker from a lost/canceled run from permanently suppressing legitimate output
5. **Non-matching live finals rendered normally**: Intervening user/system rows naturally clear the marker via `appendNonSystem()`
6. **Stale-history recovery intact**: Late displayable final allowed through after `sessions.changed` clears display-state maps
7. **All 134 tests pass**: 26 ChatLog + 81 event-handler + 13 PTY fake-backend + 14 PTY local smoke

What was not tested: Telegram channel E2E roundtrip (requires Telegram bot backend and live channel setup). The `sessions.changed` → `loadHistory()` replay → late `chat.final` race is exercised by: Gateway + TUI live proof (real binary + production Gateway + deepseek model), PTY local mode (real binary + embedded backend), PTY fake-backend (/reset triggers `sessions.changed` reload), and 81 event-handler unit tests covering the complete dedup state machine.
target.author: xialonglee

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
